name: Vintermorkret
description: A lyrical novel about isolation and longing in northern Sweden during the darkest winter months.

name: The Amber Hours
description: Three generations of women navigate love and loss across post-war Europe in this luminous debut.

name: Skogens Vittnen
description: A celebrated Swedish novel about memory and belonging, set deep in the countryside of Dalarna.

name: Briefe an das Licht
description: An epistolary novel about letters never sent across a divided Berlin, published by Albatross Verlag.

name: Still Water Running
description: Nora Clement's quiet masterpiece follows a river community grappling with an uncertain industrial future.

name: Ljusbrytning
description: Anders Lilja's debut poetry collection explores refraction of light, time, and the Swedish landscape.

name: The Salt Psalms
description: A bilingual collection by Marta Kowalska drawing on coastal imagery and the experience of migration.

name: Markta Dagar
description: Verse exploring days marked by grief and unexpected joy, in the tradition of Sara Stridsberg.

name: Drommarnas Arkiv
description: A wide-ranging essay collection about literature, dreams, and Scandinavian cultural identity.

name: The Independent Bookshop
description: An illustrated history of independent bookshops across Europe, celebrating literary culture.

name: Havspelarens Dotter
description: Sofia Nyman's second novel follows a musician's daughter uncovering family secrets on the Swedish west coast.

name: Der Stille Garten
description: A contemplative German novel about a botanical garden and the intertwined lives of its caretakers.

name: Spegelgatan
description: A debut novel tracing one Uppsala street's transformation across a full century of human lives.

name: Le Carnet Bleu
description: A slim French novel about a writer's blue notebook and the dangerous secrets concealed within its pages.

name: Tidvattnets Barn
description: Anders Lilja's second novel follows twin sisters separated by circumstance and reunited by the sea.

name: The Glass Letters
description: Nora Clement's epistolary novel set between London and Uppsala during the 1970s.

name: Kvallsljus
description: A short poetry collection by Sofia Nyman meditating on twilight, threshold moments, and belonging.

name: Das Haus am Fluss
description: A multigenerational German family saga set along the Rhine, spanning two world wars and their aftermath.

name: Reading Scandinavia
description: A critical non-fiction exploration of contemporary Nordic literature and its global reception.

name: Gryningsrosa
description: A poetry debut set in the early hours of morning, exploring vulnerability and the courage to begin.

name: The Bookseller of Svartbacksgatan
description: A lighthearted novel inspired by independent bookshops, set in a fictionalised Uppsala.

name: Vatten och Eld
description: Marta Kowalska's Swedish-language novel about a firefighter's wife piecing together what was lost.
